2606.22269.title: Evaluating Large Language Models for Hausa and Fongbe Machine Translation: Benchmarks, Failures, and Metric Reliability
2606.22269.paper_summary: We investigate the translation quality of current large language models (LLMs) for English-to-Hausa and English-to-Fongbe - two typologically distinct West African languages from the Afroasiatic and Niger-Congo families respectively - and evaluate whether standard automatic metrics reliably reflect human judgment for these low-resource languages. We evaluate four models (GPT-4o Mini, Claude Sonnet 4, Gemini 2.5 Flash, and Qwen2.5-7B) at progressive scales (500 to 10,000 sentences) using automatic metrics (BLEU, chrF++, TER, COMET, BERTScore) validated against native-speaker judgment. Our results reveal three key findings. First, translation quality varies substantially by language: Hausa achieves acceptable quality (human scores 4.0-4.5/5) while Fongbe achieves poor quality (1.0-2.2/5), with a consistent 3x BLEU gap across all systems. Second, model rankings differ by language - Gemini leads for Fongbe while GPT-4o leads for Hausa by human evaluation - indicating that performance on one low-resource African language does not predict performance on another. Third, metric-human correlation varies dramatically: perfect rank correlation for Fongbe (rho=1.0) but weak correlation for Hausa (rho=0.5), where human evaluators preferred GPT-4o despite all automatic metrics ranking Claude first. We further show that neural metrics like BERTScore exhibit embedding collapse (within-language similarity >0.99) for both languages, limiting their ability to differentiate translation quality. Based on these findings, we recommend multi-metric evaluation for low-resource African languages, with particular caution when interpreting neural metrics. We establish that minimum sample sizes of n=2,500 sentences are required for stable system rankings, as smaller samples produced artifact findings that reversed at scale.
2606.22269.paper_summary_zh: 我們調查目前大型語言模型（LLMs）在英語到豪薩語和英語到豐貝語的翻譯質量——這兩種語言分別來自亞非語系和尼日爾-剛果語系，具有顯著的類型學差異——並評估標準自動指標是否能可靠地反映這些低資源語言的人類評價。我們評估了四個模型（GPT-4o Mini、Claude Sonnet 4、Gemini 2.5 Flash 和 Qwen2.5-7B），在逐步擴展的範圍內（500 到 10,000 句）使用自動指標（BLEU、chrF++、TER、COMET、BERTScore），並與母語者的評價進行驗證。我們的結果揭示了三個關鍵發現。首先，翻譯質量因語言而異：豪薩語達到可接受的質量（人類評分 4.0-4.5/5），而豐貝語的質量較差（1.0-2.2/5），在所有系統中BLEU指標的差距始終為3倍。其次，模型的排名因語言而異——在豐貝語中，Gemini 表現最佳，而在豪薩語中，GPT-4o 表現最佳，這表明在一種低資源非洲語言上的表現無法預測另一種語言的表現。第三，指標與人類評價的相關性差異顯著：豐貝語的排名相關性完美（rho=1.0），而豪薩語的相關性較弱（rho=0.5），人類評估者更偏好 GPT-4o，儘管所有自動指標都將 Claude 排在第一。我們進一步顯示，像 BERTScore 這樣的神經指標對於這兩種語言顯示出嵌入崩潰（同語言相似度 >0.99），限制了它們區分翻譯質量的能力。根據這些發現，我們建議對低資源非洲語言進行多指標評估，並在解釋神經指標時特別謹慎。我們確定需要至少 n=2,500 句的樣本量以獲得穩定的系統排名，因為較小的樣本產生的人工結果在擴大規模時會出現反轉。
2606.22269.author: Mahounan Pericles Adjovi et.al.
2606.22269.authors: Mahounan Pericles Adjovi, Roald Eiselen, Prasenjit Mitra
